description:
Eliminate gratuitous box/unbox at the continuation boundary for fully-native call chains. Today every cont-target's slot 0 is force-Tagged module-wide as a conservative measure for the trampoline-path writers; this taxes the common native-chain case with a CLIF box at the caller and an unbox at the callee with no semantic role.

## Motivation — add1.fz CLIF

```
fn add1(n) do n + 1 end
fn main() do print(add1(41)) end
```

Current main:
```
v4 = call fn0(v3, v1)       ; add1_s2 returns raw i64
v5 = ishl_imm v4, 3         ; box step 1 (gratuitous)
v6 = bor_imm v5, 1          ; box step 2 (gratuitous)
v7 = call fn1(v6, v1)       ; k_2_s3 native call
```

Current k_2_s3:
```
v3 = sshr_imm v0, 3         ; unbox (gratuitous)
call print(v3)
```

Target shape:
```
v4 = call fn0(v3, v1)
v7 = call fn1(v4, v1)
```
and k_2_s3 receives raw int directly. No shift, no OR.

## Root cause (researched)

Two coordinated decisions force the dance:

1. **Compile-time pin** (`src/ir_codegen.rs:1440-1447, 1653-1656`): `cont_target_fns` is per-FnId; if any spec of an FnId could be reached as a cont, ALL specs of that FnId have entry-param-0 forced to Tagged. This is too coarse — the property is really per-spec and depends on which write path reaches that spec.

2. **Native-chain hop** (`src/ir_codegen.rs:2730-2757`): the native-chain branch coerces `result -> Tagged -> cont_param_reprs[0]`. With (1) pinning the destination to Tagged, the intermediate is "free." With (1) relaxed, the hop becomes box-then-unbox round-trip — so this site needs the matching local cleanup.

The unconditional-Tagged writers that motivated (1) are:
- `emit_return` (line 3081) — uniform callee's Return writes Tagged into cont slot 1 with no per-cont coercion.
- TailCall uniform branch (line 2905-2910) — writes Tagged into MY cont slot 1.
- Closure stub native-callee path (line 3366-3371) — writes Tagged into cont slot 1.

The kind-aware writer (line 2820 via `store_args_into_callee_frame`) already coerces per `FieldKind`; that path doesn't need slot 0 forced.

## Why this is tractable now

Under `.29` per-callsite specialization (per the close-comment on `.27.5.4.1`), each cont spec corresponds to a unique callee-spec return Descr by construction. The multi-callsite-agreement problem that blocked typed result slots is gone. What remains is plumbing.

## DAG

- **.32.1** Per-spec uniform-cont-reachable analysis; relax `cont_target_fns` to a per-spec set; specs reached only via native chain get Tagged-free slot 0.
- **.32.2** [blocked by .32.1] Native-chain branch: feed callee result directly to `cont_param_reprs[0]` without the Tagged intermediate. Verifies add1.fz CLIF reaches target shape.

## Out of scope (follow-up)

- Kind-aware uniform writers (`emit_return`, TailCall uniform branch, closure stub) so specs reached via uniform writeback can also relax. Requires runtime schema_id dispatch in `emit_return` (cont header already carries resolved SpecId per `.29.12.1`) or an inverse analysis. File as `.32.3` only if/when a real program demands it; not required for the add1.fz motivation.

## Three-path parity

Codegen-only changes; JIT and AOT share `compile_with_backend`. Interp unaffected (no ABI).

## Acceptance (epic)

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- add1.fz CLIF for `main` and `k_2_s3` matches the target shape above (no ishl/bor/sshr at the cont boundary).
- No regression in any existing CLIF golden / spec parity.
title: [EPIC] Minimal cont-boundary CLIF (kill gratuitous box/unbox)
